name: BEHÇET DISEASE
definition: rare multisystemic chronic relapsing inflammatory vasculitis of variable-sized vessels in develops > 10 years after onset of Behçet disease develops within 10 years after onset of Behçet disease

demographics.Histo: nonspecific necrotizing vasculitis with deposition of immune complexes in walls of
demographics.Associated with: human leukocyte antigen HLA-B51; genetic mutations including factor V
demographics.Path: lymphocytic vasculitis of venules of bowel wall → chronic inflammation + intestinal
demographics.Location: terminal ileum (most common), often together with proximal cecum

distribution: terminal ileum (most common), often together with proximal cecum
discriminating_features: downhill esophageal varices ← thrombotic occlusion of SVC → fibrosing

Joints: downhill esophageal varices ← thrombotic occlusion of SVC → fibrosing
Large intestine: multiple discrete punched-out ulcers throughout colon mimicking Crohn disease
Parenchymal Neurobehçet: isolated / confluent irregular T2 hyperintense foci of circular / linear / crescent shape
atypically space-occupying lesion masquerading as unilateral brain tumor
Nonparenchymal Neurobehçet: dural sinus thrombosis (5-25%) → raised intracranial pressure
aneurysm of sinus of Valsalva, periaortic pseudoaneurysm
Veins (25%): migratory thrombophlebitis: mediastinal widening ← edema ← thrombosis of SVC
thrombosis of IVC, veins in upper extremities
deep vein thrombosis in lower extremities
Pulmonary artery: increased perihilar radiopacity ← pulmonary artery aneurysm
pulmonary artery thromboembolism ← DVT ← hypercoagulable state
Lung: multiple subpleural wedge-shaped / ill-defined increased opacities ← areas of
ill-defined ground-glass opacities with consolidation = hemorrhage ← ruptured
